channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: NASA launches Europa Clipper to Jupiter’s ocean moon. Here’s why
item.description: A NASA spacecraft rocketed away Monday on a quest to explore Jupiter’s tantalizing moon Europa and reveal whether its vast hidden ocean might hold the keys to life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How can AI win the trust of doctors and nurses?
item.description: In August 2023, the American Medical Association surveyed over 1,000 physicians about their sentiments toward AI. The results, published at the end of that year, painted a nuanced picture.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Mahmoud, Fast Company

item.title: Apple powers up the iPad mini for Apple Intelligence
item.description: As expected, Apple has introduced a much faster Apple Intelligence-capable iPad mini equipped with the same A17 Pro chip used in the iPhone 15 Pro series. That’s a good improvement from the A15 Bionic in the previous model, and makes for faster graphics, computation, and AI calculation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: SUSE Edge upgrade targets Kubernetes and Linux at the edge
item.description: Open source and Linux platform vendor SUSE is looking to help organizations solve some of the complexity and challenges of edge computing with the company’s SUSE Edge 3.1 release, announced today.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biden admin to provide $750 million to North Carolina-based Wolfspeed for advanced computer chips
item.description: The Biden-Harris administration announced plans to provide up to $750 million in direct funding to Wolfspeed, with the money supporting its new silicon carbide factory in North Carolina that makes the wafers used in advanced computer chips and its factory in Marcy, New York.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: Hong Kong’s oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The world’s space agencies are meeting in Milan. Here’s what’s on the agenda
item.description: The world’s space agencies are meeting in Milan this week as geopolitical rivalry fuels a new global race in Earth’s orbit and on the moon, with heavy involvement from a private sector toiling to keep pace with Elon Musk’s dominant SpaceX.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Apple accused of restricting workers' Slack, social media use
item.description: The National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) filed a complaint against Apple on Thursday, accusing the iPhone maker of improperly restricting workers’ use of Slack and other social media.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Adobe makes GenStudio app generally available
item.description: Adobe’s GenStudio content supply chain platform is now generally available, with the ability to publish content directly to social media channels such as Instagram, Snap and TikTok coming soon.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows users are being tricked by sneaky malware scheme
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how security experts found new malware, Lumma Stealer, spreading via fake verification pages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI being used to run world’s first indoor vertical berry farm
item.description: First indoor vertical berry farm opens in Virginia, using AI to grow 4 million pounds annually. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson takes a closer look at the "growing" trend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Top 5 methods to equip your car with Wi-Fi
item.description: Having Wi-Fi has become a necessity for many of us. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the top five ways to equip your vehicles with Wi-Fi.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 77,000 customers' personal information is exposed in Fidelity Investments data breach
item.description: Fidelity Investments, one of the world's biggest asset managers, experienced a data breach in August, the company's second in 12 months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Connect in a crisis with iOS 18's new video call feature for emergency services
item.description: Apple’s new Emergency SOS Live Video feature allows users to share live video footage of the emergency directly with first responders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Democrats press tech leaders to curb hurricane misinformation
item.description: A group of House Democrats representing areas affected by hurricanes Helene and Milton are calling on the leaders of major technology companies to do more to combat the flurry of misinformation online about the extreme weather. In a letter sent Friday, Reps. Deborah Ross (N.C.), Kathy Castor (Fla.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Musk unveils long-awaited robotaxi
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk unveiled the electric vehicle (EV) company’s long-awaited robotaxi Thursday night, showing off a fleet of sleek self-driving cars with no steering wheels or pedals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AMD unveils new generation of Epyc, Instinct chips
item.description: AMD announced its latest AI and high-performance computing processors at its Advancing AI event in San Francisco, including the fifth generation of its Epyc server processors and AMD Instinct MI325X accelerators.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: More than one-third of cloud environments are critically exposed, says Tenable
item.description: In the first half of this year, 38% of organizations had at least one cloud workload that was critically vulnerable, highly privileged, and publicly exposed, according to a study of telemetry from customers of cloud security vendor Tenable released this week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biometric data: Is it safe to hand it over to any company that asks?
item.description: Biometric data, like face and fingerprint scans, can help you save time when accessing your personal data – but with it comes a whole host of privacy risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DoD considers faster acquisition pathway for AI
item.description: The Army is exploring the idea of creating a separate path or a sub-path within the software acquisition pathway specifically for AI. The post DoD considers faster acquisition pathway for AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI sees continued attempts to use AI models for election interference
item.description: OpenAI has seen continued attempts by cybercriminals to use its artificial intelligence (AI) models for fake content aimed at interfering with this year’s elections, the ChatGPT maker said in a new report.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Say goodbye to dead batteries with this portable wind turbine
item.description: The Shine 2.0 is a 3-in-1 portable wind turbine, charge controller and power bank that fits into a backpack and works day and night, rain or shine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft’s Drasi: A lightweight approach to event-driven programming
item.description: The Microsoft Azure Incubations team is one of the more interesting components of Microsoft’s hyperscale cloud. It is something between a traditional software development team and a research organization working to build solutions to the problems of distributed systems at massive scale.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Electron vs. Tauri: Which cross-platform framework is for you?
item.description: If there’s one thing developers agree on about cross-platform applications, it’s that it’s easier to create a GUI with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript than to use one of the many cross-platform frameworks out there. Frameworks like Electron give you the best of both worlds.
item.dc:creator: Info World
